body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates documentation and supporting artifacts for the project’s E2E testing workflow and “AI Development Lab” narrative, while also introducing a regression test + code fix for an EMHASS edge case (deferrable total hours exceeding the available charging window) and adjusting HA startup ordering around the coordinator/EMHASS publish.

**Changes:**
- Add/update extensive docs: E2E testing guides, analysis (EN/ES), AI lab/portfolio materials, roadmap/todo/release notes, and research notes.
- Add unit tests demonstrating/covering the `def_total_hours` vs available window mismatch, plus a corresponding fix in `emhass_adapter.py`.
- Update HA init sequence and frontend EMHASS Jinja template output.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 53 out of 55 changed files in this pull request and generated 10 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| ui-map.local.es.md | Added local UI selector map for manual/E2E verification (includes login flow notes). |
| tests/test_def_total_hours_window_mismatch.py | New unit tests for `def_total_hours` exceeding the available window scenario. |
| test-analysis.md | Updated E2E test analysis document (translated/rewritten to EN). |
| test-analysis.es.md | Added Spanish version of the E2E test analysis. |
| specs/emhass-integration-with-fixes/.progress.md | Progress notes updated with startup/race-condition learnings and file pointers. |
| specs/.index/index.md | Updated specs index timestamp and entry for `e2e-ux-tests-fix`. |
| specs/.index/index-state.json | Updated index state timestamp and simplified phase metadata for `e2e-ux-tests-fix`. |
| research_bug_hypothesis_entry_data_options.md | Added research note about ConfigEntry `data` vs `options` storage for EMHASS values. |
| playwright-env.local.es.md | Added a local Playwright environment reference file (Spanish). |
| plans/winston-architect-review-ai-dev-lab.md | Added architecture review notes for the AI lab documentation set. |
| plans/unified-change-plan.md | Added a consolidated documentation improvement plan. |
| docs/index.md | Updated documentation index with AI lab/portfolio links and updated test counts. |
| docs/e2e-date-diagnosis-final.md | Converted/updated E2E date diagnosis doc to EN. |
| docs/e2e-date-diagnosis-final.es.md | Added Spanish version of E2E date diagnosis. |
| docs/TESTING_E2E.es.md | Added Spanish E2E testing guide for running Playwright locally/CI. |
| docs/RALPH_METHODOLOGY.es.md | Added Spanish Smart Ralph methodology documentation. |
| docs/PORTFOLIO.md | Added portfolio/overview doc positioning the project and methodology. |
| docs/MILESTONE_4_POWER_PROFILE.es.md | Added/updated Spanish milestone documentation for power profile behavior. |
| docs/MILESTONE_4_1_PLANNING.es.md | Added/updated Spanish planning doc for milestone 4.1 (future work). |
| doc/gaps/promptspecrfactor.md | Added prompt/spec guidance for SOLID refactor + 100% coverage work. |
| doc/gaps/promptspecrfactor.es.md | Added Spanish version of the SOLID refactor prompt/spec guidance. |
| doc/gaps/EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md | Added executive summary for technical debt/gaps. |
| custom_components/ev_trip_planner/frontend/panel.js | Updated EMHASS Jinja2 template section to include additional parameters and reference table updates. |
| custom_components/ev_trip_planner/emhass_adapter.py | Added a cap so `def_total_hours` does not exceed the computed available window. |
| custom_components/ev_trip_planner/__init__.py | Changed startup order: create/refresh coordinator before publishing trips to EMHASS. |
| TODO.md | Translated/updated TODO/backlog content to EN. |
| TODO.es.md | Added Spanish TODO/backlog file. |
| ROADMAP.es.md | Added Spanish roadmap/milestones document. |
| RELEASE_NOTES_v0.5.9.md | Reformatted/translated release notes to EN with headings. |
| RELEASE_NOTES_v0.5.9.es.md | Added Spanish release notes for v0.5.9. |
| RELEASE_NOTES_v0.5.17.md | Partially translated headings/content to EN. |
| RELEASE_NOTES_v0.5.17.es.md | Added Spanish release notes for v0.5.17. |
| RELEASE_NOTES_v0.5.16.md | Partially translated headings/content to EN. |
| RELEASE_NOTES_v0.5.16.es.md | Added Spanish release notes for v0.5.16. |
| README.md | Added link/intro banner pointing readers to the Portfolio doc. |
| CLAUDE.es.md | Added Spanish CLAUDE/BMAD integration instructions file. |
| CHANGELOG.md | Translated parts of changelog entries to EN. |
| .research/restart_empty_sensor_bug.md | Added research note analyzing empty EMHASS sensor data after HA restart. |
</details>

**Actionable comments posted: 6** > [!NOTE] > Due to the large number of review comments, Critical, Major severity comments were prioritized as inline comments. > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (5)
> >
> CLAUDE.es.md (1)
> > `1-42`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ > > **Language-filename mismatch: English content in `.es.md` file.** > > The filename `CLAUDE.es.md` uses the `.es` suffix, which conventionally indicates Spanish (Español) content, but the entire file is written in English. This creates confusion about the intended audience and language. > > Please rename the file to match its content language (e.g., `CLAUDE.md`) or translate the content to Spanish if the `.es` suffix is intentional. > > > > **Note on static analysis hints:** > The LanguageTool suggestions about "GitHub" capitalization are false positives—they refer to the `.github/` directory path, not the platform name. The repetitive "Use" on line 6 is acceptable for technical documentation clarity. > >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@CLAUDE.es.md` around lines 1 - 42, The file CLAUDE.es.md contains English > content but uses the .es suffix; either rename the file from CLAUDE.es.md to > CLAUDE.md (and update any references) or translate the content to Spanish > (keeping the filename CLAUDE.es.md); ensure internal links like > `.github/copilot-instructions.md` and `_bmad/COMMANDS.md` remain correct after > the change. > ``` > >
> >
>
> CONTRIBUTING.md (1)
> > `68-91`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ > > **Update CONTRIBUTING.md tool configurations to match current targets.** > > Lines 72, 79, and 85 in CONTRIBUTING.md document `py311`/`3.11` across all tools, but `pyproject.toml` shows the project now targets Python 3.14 for mypy. Update the documented configuration to reflect the actual current state—mypy targets 3.14 while Black and Pylint remain on 3.11—to prevent contributor misconfiguration. > >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@CONTRIBUTING.md` around lines 68 - 91, The documented tool configuration in > CONTRIBUTING.md is inconsistent: update the mypy Python version reference (the > `python_version = "3.11"` entry under `[tool.mypy]` and any mention of > `py311`/`3.11` that specifically refers to mypy) to reflect the actual project > target of Python 3.14 (`python_version = "3.14"`), while leaving Black's > `target-version = ['py311']` and Pylint's `py-version = "3.11"` entries > unchanged; ensure only the `[tool.mypy]` block and any surrounding explanatory > text are changed so contributors configure mypy for 3.14 without altering Black > or Pylint settings. > ``` > >
> >
>
> specs/.index/index-state.json (1)
> > `36-39`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_ > > **Critical: malformed JSON — stray `0` breaks parsing for `009-fix-vehicle-creation-dashboard-notifications`.** > > Line 37 is `"taskIndex": 0` and line 38 is a lone `0,`, producing an invalid token sequence (`0 0,`). `json.loads` will reject this file, breaking `.ralph/scripts/merge_state.py`, `reset_state.py`, and the index generator. The same corruption is visible in `specs/.index/index.md` lines 21-22 (`| ... | tasks | 0\n0/158 tasks |`), which is the downstream symptom of this root cause. > > The same pattern also appears at lines 66-68 for `017-native-panel-core` (and shows up in `index.md` at lines 26-27). > > > >
> 🔧 Proposed fix > > ```diff > "name": "009-fix-vehicle-creation-dashboard-notifications", > "path": "./specs/009-fix-vehicle-creation-dashboard-notifications", > "phase": "tasks", > - "taskIndex": 0 > -0, > + "taskIndex": 0, > "totalTasks": 158 > }, > ``` > > And symmetrically at lines 66-68: > > ```diff > "name": "017-native-panel-core", > "path": "./specs/017-native-panel-core", > "phase": "tasks", > - "taskIndex": 0 > -0, > + "taskIndex": 0, > "totalTasks": 68 > }, > ``` > > After fixing, regenerate `index.md` so the "Status" cells render `0/158 tasks` and `0/68 tasks` on a single line. >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@specs/.index/index-state.json` around lines 36 - 39, The JSON is malformed > because a stray literal "0," appears immediately after the "taskIndex": 0 entry, > producing an invalid token sequence; open the file and remove the extraneous > standalone 0 entries so each object contains only the "taskIndex" and > "totalTasks" properties (fix the identical stray 0 at the block containing > "017-native-panel-core" as well), then regenerate the index markdown so the > status cells render as "0/158 tasks" and "0/68 tasks" on a single line; verify > with a json parser that the file now loads cleanly and that "taskIndex" and > "totalTasks" remain correct. > ``` > >
> >
>
> custom_components/ev_trip_planner/frontend/panel.js (1)
> > `1061-1076`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ > > **Reference table is missing the new `set_deferrable_startup_penalty` row.** > > The Jinja2 template at lines 964-978 emits three new parameters, but the documentation table only adds two rows (`treat_deferrable_load_as_semi_cont`, `set_deferrable_load_single_constant`). Add a matching row for `set_deferrable_startup_penalty` so the "EMHASS Parameters Reference" stays in sync with what the template actually publishes. > > Also worth aligning the "all 8 EMHASS parameters" copy on Line 998 with the actual count now exposed in the template/table. > >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@custom_components/ev_trip_planner/frontend/panel.js` around lines 1061 - > 1076, Add a missing documentation table row for the new parameter > set_deferrable_startup_penalty: insert a entry matching the style of the > other rows with the name set_deferrable_startup_penalty, a short > description (e.g., "Startup penalty applied when a deferrable load is started"), > and the example/value source (e.g., [value] * > number_of_deferrable_loads or > emhass.attributes.set_deferrable_startup_penalty), and update the > copy that currently says "all 8 EMHASS parameters" to reflect the new total > (change to "all 9 EMHASS parameters") so the table and descriptive text match > the Jinja2 template output. > ``` > >
> >
>
> ROADMAP.md (1)
> > `143-145`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ > > **Invalid YAML example: duplicate `p_deferrable` key will overwrite values.** > > At Line 143-145, the same mapping key is declared twice, so only one value survives in YAML parsers. This will misconfigure EMHASS if users copy it as-is. > > >
> Proposed fix > > ```diff > P_deferrable: > - p_deferrable: "{{ state_attr('sensor.ev_trip_planner_coche_viaje_1', 'power_profile_watts') | default([]) }}" > - p_deferrable: "{{ state_attr('sensor.ev_trip_planner_coche_viaje_2', 'power_profile_watts') | default([]) }}" > + - "{{ state_attr('sensor.ev_trip_planner_coche_viaje_1', 'power_profile_watts') | default([]) }}" > + - "{{ state_attr('sensor.ev_trip_planner_coche_viaje_2', 'power_profile_watts') | default([]) }}" > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@ROADMAP.md` around lines 143 - 145, The YAML snippet defines P_deferrable > with duplicate p_deferrable keys which causes the second to overwrite the first; > fix by replacing the two p_deferrable entries with a single p_deferrable mapping > that either (a) merges/concatenates the power_profile_watts values from > sensor.ev_trip_planner_coche_viaje_1 and sensor.ev_trip_planner_coche_viaje_2 > into one list, or (b) rename the duplicate keys to unique identifiers (e.g., > p_deferrable_1 and p_deferrable_2) and update any code reading P_deferrable > accordingly; locate the block using the P_deferrable mapping and the state_attr > references to the two sensors to implement the merge or renaming. > ``` > >
> >
> >
🟡 Minor comments (19)
CONTRIBUTING.md-17-17 (1)
`17-17`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Add language identifiers to fenced code blocks.** These fences are missing language tags (MD040), which will keep markdownlint warnings active.
Suggested doc fix ```diff -``` +```text custom_components/ └── ev_trip_planner/ ... -``` +``` -``` +```text feat: new functionality fix: bug fix ... -``` +``` ```
Also applies to: 458-458
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ```` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@CONTRIBUTING.md` at line 17, Update the fenced code blocks in CONTRIBUTING.md to include language identifiers (e.g., change ``` to ```text) for the snippet starting with "custom_components/ └── ev_trip_planner/ ..." and the commit-type list snippet that begins with "feat: new functionality" so markdownlint MD040 warnings are resolved; locate each triple-backtick fence around those blocks and add an appropriate language tag (text) to the opening fence. ````
CONTRIBUTING.es.md-17-17 (1)
`17-17`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Añade lenguaje a bloques de código sin especificar (MD040).** Estos fences sin lenguaje mantienen warnings de markdownlint activos.
Propuesta de ajuste ```diff -``` +```text custom_components/ ... -``` +``` -``` +```text feat: nueva funcionalidad fix: corrección de bug ... -``` +``` -``` +```text homeassistant>=2024.1.0 pytest>=7.0.0 ... -``` +``` ```
Also applies to: 458-458, 548-548
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ```` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@CONTRIBUTING.es.md` at line 17, Los bloques de código sin especificar lenguaje (MD040) deben recibir una etiqueta para silenciar warnings; en los tres fences mostrados reemplaza cada "```" por "```text" (por ejemplo los bloques que contienen "custom_components/ ...", el que contiene "feat: nueva funcionalidad\nfix: corrección de bug ..." y el que contiene "homeassistant>=2024.1.0\npytest>=7.0.0 ..."), y corrige las otras instancias sin lenguaje mencionadas (las ocurrencias alrededor de 458 y 548) para usar "```text". ````
CONTRIBUTING.es.md-20-25 (1)
`20-25`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Corrige el árbol: `__init__.py` aparece duplicado.** En la estructura de `custom_components/ev_trip_planner/` se lista `__init__.py` dos veces (Line 20 y Line 24), lo que puede confundir a nuevos contribuidores.
Propuesta de ajuste ```diff custom_components/ └── ev_trip_planner/ - ├── __init__.py # Entry point de la integración + ├── __init__.py # Entry point + Coordinator logic ├── manifest.json # Metadata (REQUERIDO) ├── const.py # Constantes globales ├── config_flow.py # Configuración UI (opcional pero recomendado) - ├── __init__.py # Entry point + Coordinator logic ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@CONTRIBUTING.es.md` around lines 20 - 25, La entrada duplicada "__init__.py" en el árbol (aparece como "Entry point de la integración" y otra vez como "Entry point + Coordinator logic") debe corregirse: elimina una de las dos líneas y conserva una sola "__init__.py" con la descripción correcta (por ejemplo "Entry point + Coordinator logic"), asegurándote de que los nombres únicos listados sean: __init__.py, manifest.json, const.py, config_flow.py, y cualquier otra carpeta/archivo; actualiza el bloque del árbol de archivos para que no muestre duplicados y refleje la descripción consolidada. ```
CONTRIBUTING.md-20-25 (1)
`20-25`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Remove duplicated `__init__.py` entry in the integration tree.** Line 20 and Line 24 both list `__init__.py` under the same folder, which makes the structure diagram inaccurate for contributors.
Suggested doc fix ```diff custom_components/ └── ev_trip_planner/ - ├── __init__.py # Integration entry point + ├── __init__.py # Entry point + Coordinator logic ├── manifest.json # Metadata (REQUIRED) ├── const.py # Global constants ├── config_flow.py # UI configuration (optional but recommended) - ├── __init__.py # Entry point + Coordinator logic ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@CONTRIBUTING.md` around lines 20 - 25, The integration tree lists __init__.py twice causing a duplicated entry; edit the CONTRIBUTING.md tree so each file appears only once (remove the redundant "__init__.py" line under the same folder), leaving a single __init__.py entry alongside manifest.json, const.py, and config_flow.py to accurately reflect the directory structure. ```
docs/TDD_METHODOLOGY.es.md-349-349 (1)
`349-349`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Alinea el ejemplo de `patch()` con la regla de boundaries externos.** Line 349 usa `patch('custom_components.ev_trip_planner.services.handle_trip_create')`, que parchea código interno de la integración y contradice la propia norma del documento.
Propuesta de ajuste ```diff -| **Patch** | Reemplazar temporalmente atributos/objetos en scope de módulo | Usar `patch()` solo en boundaries (llamadas a subsistemas HA), no dentro de tu código | `patch('custom_components.ev_trip_planner.services.handle_trip_create')` | +| **Patch** | Reemplazar temporalmente atributos/objetos en scope de módulo | Usar `patch()` solo en boundaries (subsistemas externos/HA), no dentro de tu código | `patch('homeassistant.helpers.storage.Store')` | ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@docs/TDD_METHODOLOGY.es.md` at line 349, La línea usa patch('custom_components.ev_trip_planner.services.handle_trip_create'), lo cual parchea código interno y rompe la regla de boundaries; cambia el ejemplo para parchear el boundary externo que la integración llama (p. ej. el servicio o cliente de Home Assistant que la integración invoca) en lugar de la función interna handle_trip_create, actualiza el texto para mostrar patch() apuntando al límite externo correspondiente y ajusta la explicación para dejar claro que solo se parchean boundaries (llamadas a subsistemas HA), no funciones internas del componente. ```
playwright-env.local.es.md-1-1 (1)
`1-1`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Filename/title mismatch and unclear localization intent.** The file is named `playwright-env.local.es.md` (Spanish suffix) but the content — including the title on Line 1 (`playwright-env.local.md`) — is entirely in English. Either rename the file to drop the `.es` suffix, or translate the content so it serves as the Spanish counterpart to an English version. The mismatch also makes grep/tooling keyed on the `.es.md` convention misleading.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@playwright-env.local.es.md` at line 1, The file named "playwright-env.local.es.md" has an English title "playwright-env.local.md" and English content, causing a filename/title localization mismatch; fix by either renaming the file to remove the ".es" suffix (make it "playwright-env.local.md") and update any references/tooling that expect the .es suffix, or translate the file content and the title into Spanish so it is a true Spanish counterpart (keep the filename "playwright-env.local.es.md"); ensure any grep/tooling patterns and cross-references are updated accordingly. ```
CHANGELOG.es.md-195-195 (1)
`195-195`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Corrupted text: Chinese characters embedded in Spanish line.** Line 195 reads `Tests para验证ar fixes` — "验证" (Chinese for "verify") has been inserted into the middle of the intended Spanish word, most likely by an autocorrect / IME switch. It should be `Tests para verificar fixes`.
✏️ Proposed fix ```diff - - `tests/test_config_flow_issues.py` - Tests para验证ar fixes + - `tests/test_config_flow_issues.py` - Tests para verificar fixes ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@CHANGELOG.es.md` at line 195, Replace the corrupted text in the CHANGELOG entry that currently reads "Tests para验证ar fixes" with the correct Spanish wording "Tests para verificar fixes"; locate the line containing the snippet `tests/test_config_flow_issues.py` and update the phrase so the line becomes `Tests para verificar fixes`. ```
test-analysis.md-117-131 (1)
`117-131`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Wrong emoji for "Green Phase" and "Refactor Phase".** Both section headings use `🟻` (brown square). The Green phase should use `🟢` (green circle), and it's conventional to use a distinct marker (e.g., `🔵`) for the Refactor phase.
✏️ Proposed fix ```diff -### 🟻 Green Phase: Next +### 🟢 Green Phase: Next ... -### 🟻 Refactor Phase: Final +### 🔵 Refactor Phase: Final ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@test-analysis.md` around lines 117 - 131, The headings use the wrong emoji marker; update the "### 🟻 Green Phase: Next" heading to use a green circle emoji "🟢" and change the "### 🟻 Refactor Phase: Final" heading to a distinct refactor marker such as blue circle "🔵". Locate the two headings (text matching "### 🟻 Green Phase: Next" and "### 🟻 Refactor Phase: Final") and replace their leading emoji only, keeping the rest of the heading text unchanged. ```
docs/e2e-date-diagnosis-final.md-142-142 (1)
`142-142`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Fallback example has duplicated key lookup.** At Line 142, `trip.get("day")` is repeated in the same expression. If this is meant to document fallback behavior, one branch should reference a different key; otherwise the snippet is misleading.
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@docs/e2e-date-diagnosis-final.md` at line 142, The fallback example repeats trip.get("day") twice; replace the duplicate trip.get("day") with the intended alternate key (for example trip.get("date") or trip.get("day_of_month")) so the fallback order uses trip.get("day"), then trip.get("day_of_week"), then the correct third key; update the expression where trip.get("day") and trip.get("day_of_week") appear to reflect the proper third-key fallback. ```
.research/restart_empty_sensor_bug.md-21-25 (1)
`21-25`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Use repo-relative paths instead of machine-local absolute paths.** At Line 21-25, absolute `/mnt/...` paths are environment-specific and make the document hard to use for others.
Proposed fix ```diff -| `__init__.py` | `/mnt/bunker_data/ha-ev-trip-planner/ha-ev-trip-planner/custom_components/ev_trip_planner/__init__.py` | +| `__init__.py` | `custom_components/ev_trip_planner/__init__.py` | ``` (Apply similarly to the other rows.)
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In @.research/restart_empty_sensor_bug.md around lines 21 - 25, The table uses machine-local absolute paths (e.g., /mnt/bunker_data/ha-ev-trip-planner/...) which are environment-specific; update each row (files like __init__.py, trip_manager.py, emhass_adapter.py, coordinator.py, sensor.py) to use repo-relative paths instead (for example paths relative to the repository root or to the custom_components/ev_trip_planner directory) so the document is portable and not tied to your local filesystem. ```
docs/CODEGUIDELINESia.md-15-28 (1)
`15-28`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Gap table rows are malformed and likely render incorrectly.** At Line 17-28, rows start with an extra empty column (`| | G-01 ...`), which breaks table structure and readability in the primary guidelines doc.
Proposed fix ```diff -| | G-01 | Entity Identity | 🔴 Critical | Duplicate sensors on reinstall | +| G-01 | Entity Identity | 🔴 Critical | Duplicate sensors on reinstall | ``` (Apply the same pattern for G-02 … G-12.)
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@docs/CODEGUIDELINESia.md` around lines 15 - 28, The table rows for G-01…G-12 have an extra empty leading column (each row starts with "| | G-01 ..."), which breaks rendering; remove the extra initial pipe/empty cell so each row begins with a single leading pipe and the ID column contains the ID (e.g., change "| | G-01 | Entity Identity ..." to "| G-01 | Entity Identity ...") and apply the same fix for entries G-02 through G-12 to restore proper Markdown table structure. ```
docs/CODEGUIDELINESia.md-430-430 (1)
`430-430`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Conflicting threshold for `__init__.py` size rule.** Line 430 requires `__init__.py` to be `<150 lines`, while Line 584 checks for `<200 lines`. Please unify to one limit to avoid contradictory review outcomes. Also applies to: 584-584
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@docs/CODEGUIDELINESia.md` at line 430, The CODEGUIDELINESia.md contains conflicting limits for rule "R-10 · `__init__.py` Lifecycle Only" (one occurrence at "R-10" shows <150 lines while another shows <200 lines); pick the intended threshold (e.g., 150 or 200) and make both occurrences of the R-10 rule text consistent by updating the second instance (and any other duplicates) to the chosen limit so the document enforces a single, unified line-count constraint for `__init__.py`. ```
ROADMAP.md-283-295 (1)
`283-295`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ **Add a language tag to fenced code block.** At Line 283, the fenced block has no language identifier (`MD040`).
Proposed fix ```diff -``` +```text docs/ ├── MILESTONE_4_1_PLANNING.md # Detailed Milestone 4.1 plan (not started) ... -``` +``` ```
🤖 Prompt for AI Agents ```` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.
